_note: Simplified movement/breathing pacers for the Tai Chi tab, rendered by the SHARED form-model engine (web/js/formModel.js). Normalized from the per-phase authoring schema into the canonical views+phases schema by scripts/normalize_incoming.py. A gentle tempo/breath guide, not instructed Tai Chi form.
disclaimer: A simplified breathing-and-tempo pacer — move slowly through a comfortable, full range. Not a substitute for instructed Tai Chi form. Stop for chest pain, breathlessness, dizziness, or new joint pain.

movements.name: Gather & Part the Clouds

cues.Lift: Breathe in - float both hands up to chest height, palms easing upward.
cues.Part: Keep breathing in - open the arms wide and high, like parting soft clouds.
cues.Gather: Breathe out - draw the hands down and together toward the belly.

staticCues: Breathe in - float both hands up to chest height, palms easing upward.
Keep breathing in - open the arms wide and high, like parting soft clouds.
Breathe out - draw the hands down and together toward the belly.
movements.primary_benefit: Slow, wide breathing paired to opening and gathering the arms, for relaxation and psychological wellbeing associated with regular Tai Chi practice (R09).
movements.notes: Stay tall - do not sink the hips. Breathe in as the arms open and rise, out as they gather and lower. A simplified breath pacer, not instructed form; get clinician/PAR-Q+ clearance if older, deconditioned, pregnant, or living with a condition.

movements.name: Ball-Holding Circles

cues.Top: Breathe in - cradle the ball at the top of a slow vertical circle.
cues.Front: Roll the ball forward and down, soft and round.
cues.Bottom: Breathe out - the ball reaches the low point of the circle.
cues.Back: Draw the ball up and back to begin again.

staticCues: Breathe in - cradle the ball at the top of a slow vertical circle.
Roll the ball forward and down, soft and round.
Breathe out - the ball reaches the low point of the circle.
Draw the ball up and back to begin again.
movements.primary_benefit: Gentle shoulder and arm range-of-motion, in the spirit of the low-impact movement used in the Tai Chi for Arthritis program (R13).
movements.notes: High, easy stance; let the shoulder, not the back, do the work. Breathe slowly and continuously around each circle. A mobility pacer, not instructed form; seek clinician/PAR-Q+ clearance if older, deconditioned, pregnant, or with a condition.

movements.name: Gentle Spinal Twist

cues.Center: Stand tall and centered, arms resting loose.
cues.Twist Left: Breathe out - let both arms swing gently to the left, eyes following.
cues.Twist Right: Breathe out - swing the arms gently to the right; keep hips soft.

staticCues: Stand tall and centered, arms resting loose.
Breathe out - let both arms swing gently to the left, eyes following.
Breathe out - swing the arms gently to the right; keep hips soft.
movements.primary_benefit: Gentle rotational range-of-motion through the trunk, consistent with the easy mobility work of the Tai Chi for Arthritis program (R13).
movements.notes: Keep the stance tall and the twist small and comfortable; let the arms swing freely. Exhale softly into each turn. A mobility pacer, not instructed form; get clinician/PAR-Q+ clearance if older, deconditioned, pregnant, or with a condition.

movements.name: Forward-Back Weight Shift

cues.Shift Back: Breathe out - settle the weight back, feet planted, knee softly bent.
cues.Shift Forward: Breathe in - glide the weight forward over the front foot, staying tall.

staticCues: Breathe out - settle the weight back, feet planted, knee softly bent.
Breathe in - glide the weight forward over the front foot, staying tall.
movements.primary_benefit: Forward-back weight-transfer practice for balance; regular Tai Chi practice reduces falls in older adults (R01, R02, R14). This single movement is one component, not independently tested.
movements.notes: Feet stay planted; keep the stance high and the knee softly bent. Breathe in shifting forward, out settling back. Use a wall or chair nearby if unsteady. A balance pacer, not instructed form; get clinician/PAR-Q+ clearance if older, deconditioned, pregnant, or with a condition.

movements.name: Supported Single-Leg Stand

cues.Settle: Stand tall, one hand resting lightly on the wall.
cues.Lift & Hold: Breathe slow - shift onto the left leg, float the right foot up, and hold.
cues.Hold: Hold steady and tall — keep breathing slowly; never hold your breath.

staticCues: Breathe slow - shift onto the left leg, float the right foot up, and hold.
Hold steady and tall — keep breathing slowly; never hold your breath.
Stand tall, one hand resting lightly on the wall.
movements.primary_benefit: Single-leg standing balance with support; static and dynamic balance work of this kind is part of Tai Chi programs that reduce falls in older adults (R01, R02, R14).
movements.notes: Keep one hand on a wall or sturdy chair throughout. Lift the foot only as high as is comfortable and breathe slowly while you hold. Lower if you feel unsteady. A balance pacer, not instructed form; get clinician/PAR-Q+ clearance if older, deconditioned, pregnant, or with a condition.

movements.name: Tandem (Heel-to-Toe) Stand

cues.Settle: Stand with feet hip-width, hand near the wall, arms easy.
cues.Tandem Hold: Breathe slow - bring the feet into a narrow line, arms out, and hold steady.
cues.Hold: Hold steady and tall — keep breathing slowly; never hold your breath.

staticCues: Breathe slow - bring the feet into a narrow line, arms out, and hold steady.
Hold steady and tall — keep breathing slowly; never hold your breath.
Stand with feet hip-width, hand near the wall, arms easy.
movements.primary_benefit: Narrow heel-to-toe standing to challenge balance; this kind of stance work features in Tai Chi programs shown to reduce falls in older adults (R01, R14).
movements.notes: Keep a hand within reach of a wall or chair. Bring the feet into a narrow line only as far as feels safe; arms out helps balance. Breathe slowly and hold. A balance pacer, not instructed form; get clinician/PAR-Q+ clearance if older, deconditioned, pregnant, or with a condition.

movements.name: Knee Raises with Support

cues.Stand: Breathe out - stand tall, one hand resting on the wall.
cues.Knee Up: Breathe in - lift the near knee up and forward; alternate legs each cycle.

staticCues: Breathe out - stand tall, one hand resting on the wall.
Breathe in - lift the near knee up and forward; alternate legs each cycle.
movements.primary_benefit: Standing single-leg balance and gentle hip control with support; balance work of this type is part of falls-prevention Tai Chi programs (R01, R14).
movements.notes: Keep one hand on the wall. Raise the knee only to a comfortable height, stay tall, and alternate legs each breath cycle. Breathe in lifting, out lowering. A balance pacer, not instructed form; get clinician/PAR-Q+ clearance if older, deconditioned, pregnant, or with a condition.

movements.name: Heel & Toe Raises

cues.Heels Up: Breathe in - rise onto the balls of the feet, growing tall, hand on the wall.
cues.Toes Up: Breathe out - rock back onto the heels, lifting the toes.

staticCues: Breathe in - rise onto the balls of the feet, growing tall, hand on the wall.
Breathe out - rock back onto the heels, lifting the toes.
movements.primary_benefit: Activates the lower-leg muscles that support standing balance; lower-limb work of this kind is part of falls-prevention Tai Chi programs (R01, R14).
movements.notes: Hold a wall or chair for light support. Rise smoothly onto the balls of the feet, then rock back onto the heels - no bouncing. Breathe in rising, out rocking back. A movement pacer, not instructed form; get clinician/PAR-Q+ clearance if older, deconditioned, pregnant, or with a condition.

movements.name: Commencement

cues.Rise: Breathe in - float both hands up to shoulder height, wrists soft.
cues.Press: Breathe out - press the hands gently down to the hips, sinking just slightly.

staticCues: Breathe in - float both hands up to shoulder height, wrists soft.
Breathe out - press the hands gently down to the hips, sinking just slightly.
movements.primary_benefit: The opening breath of the form - slow paced breathing for relaxation and psychological wellbeing associated with regular Tai Chi practice (R09).
movements.notes: Stay tall with only a slight sink on the press; let the breath lead the hands. Breathe in as the hands rise, out as they press down. A breath pacer, not instructed form; get clinician/PAR-Q+ clearance if older, deconditioned, pregnant, or with a condition.

movements.name: Parting the Wild Horse's Mane

cues.Hold Ball: Breathe out - sit the weight back, the lead hand cradling a ball in front.
cues.Part Bow: Breathe in - shift forward and sweep the lead hand up and out; alternate sides.

staticCues: Breathe out - sit the weight back, the lead hand cradling a ball in front.
Breathe in - shift forward and sweep the lead hand up and out; alternate sides.
movements.primary_benefit: Whole-body coordination and weight transfer in a high stance; practiced as part of a Tai Chi program, this style of movement is linked to reduced falls in older adults (R01, R02).
movements.notes: Keep the stance high and knee-sparing; shift smoothly and alternate sides each cycle. Breathe in opening and shifting forward, out gathering back. A simplified pacer, not instructed form; get clinician/PAR-Q+ clearance if older, deconditioned, pregnant, or with a condition.

movements.name: Brush Knee & Push

cues.Chamber: Breathe in - sit back, drawing the pushing hand up beside the ear.
cues.Brush & Push: Breathe out - shift forward and press the palm slowly ahead; alternate sides.

staticCues: Breathe in - sit back, drawing the pushing hand up beside the ear.
Breathe out - shift forward and press the palm slowly ahead; alternate sides.
movements.primary_benefit: Coordinated step-and-push weight transfer; performed within a Tai Chi program, movement of this kind is associated with reduced falls in older adults (R01, R02).
movements.notes: High stance, front knee softly bent over the foot; press the palm slowly and alternate sides. Breathe in chambering, out pushing. A simplified pacer, not instructed form; get clinician/PAR-Q+ clearance if older, deconditioned, pregnant, or with a condition.

movements.name: Wave Hands Like Clouds

cues.Cloud Left: Breathe in - shift left as the hands circle, upper hand high on the left.
cues.Cloud Right: Breathe out - shift right, the hands trading places, upper hand high on the right.

staticCues: Breathe in - shift left as the hands circle, upper hand high on the left.
Breathe out - shift right, the hands trading places, upper hand high on the right.
movements.primary_benefit: Continuous side-to-side weight transfer with circling arms for coordination; as part of a Tai Chi practice this style of movement is linked to reduced falls (R01, R02, R14).
movements.notes: Keep a high, even stance and let the waist turn the arms. Breathe slowly and continuously as the weight flows side to side. A simplified pacer, not instructed form; get clinician/PAR-Q+ clearance if older, deconditioned, pregnant, or with a condition.

movements.name: Repulse the Monkey

cues.Open Back: Breathe in - reach the forward hand out as the other opens back.
cues.Push & Retreat: Breathe out - settle the weight back and press the palm forward; alternate sides.

staticCues: Breathe in - reach the forward hand out as the other opens back.
Breathe out - settle the weight back and press the palm forward; alternate sides.
movements.primary_benefit: Backward-stepping coordination and weight transfer; practiced within a Tai Chi program, movement of this kind is associated with reduced falls in older adults (R01, R02).
movements.notes: Step back only onto stable ground and stay tall; alternate hands and sides. Breathe in opening, out pressing and settling back. A simplified pacer, not instructed form; get clinician/PAR-Q+ clearance if older, deconditioned, pregnant, or with a condition.

movements.name: Grasp the Sparrow's Tail (Simplified)

cues.Ward & Press: Breathe in - shift forward and press both hands gently ahead.
cues.Rollback: Breathe out - sit back, drawing the hands down and back in a soft arc.

staticCues: Breathe in - shift forward and press both hands gently ahead.
Breathe out - sit back, drawing the hands down and back in a soft arc.
movements.primary_benefit: A four-part form compressed to a simple ward-and-rollback weight transfer for coordination; as part of a Tai Chi practice this style of movement is linked to reduced falls (R01, R02).
movements.notes: Simplified to two phases - not the full form. Keep the stance high and shift smoothly. Breathe in pressing forward, out rolling back. A simplified pacer, not instructed form; get clinician/PAR-Q+ clearance if older, deconditioned, pregnant, or with a condition.

movements.name: Golden Rooster Stands on One Leg (Supported)

cues.Settle: Stand tall, one hand resting on the wall.
cues.Rise & Hold: Breathe slow - lift the near knee high and raise the same-side hand; hold and balance.
cues.Hold: Hold steady and tall — keep breathing slowly; never hold your breath.

staticCues: Breathe slow - lift the near knee high and raise the same-side hand; hold and balance.
Hold steady and tall — keep breathing slowly; never hold your breath.
Stand tall, one hand resting on the wall.
movements.primary_benefit: Single-leg balance with a raised knee and arm, performed with support; balance work of this kind is part of Tai Chi programs that reduce falls in older adults (R01, R02, R14).
movements.notes: Keep one hand on a wall or chair. Lift the knee only as high as is comfortable; raise the same-side hand and breathe slowly while you hold. Lower if unsteady. A balance pacer, not instructed form; get clinician/PAR-Q+ clearance if older, deconditioned, pregnant, or with a condition.

movements.name: Seated Cloud Hands

cues.Up: Breathe in - sitting tall, float the hand up and across in a slow cloud.
cues.Across: Carry the hand smoothly across the body.
cues.Down: Breathe out - let the hand sink softly downward to begin again.

staticCues: Breathe in - sitting tall, float the hand up and across in a slow cloud.
Carry the hand smoothly across the body.
Breathe out - let the hand sink softly downward to begin again.
movements.primary_benefit: Seated shoulder and trunk range-of-motion, in the spirit of the gentle, joint-friendly movement of the Tai Chi for Arthritis program (R13).
movements.notes: Sit tall toward the front of a sturdy chair, feet flat. Let the hand float in slow clouds within a comfortable range. Breathe in rising, out sinking. A seated mobility pacer, not instructed form; get clinician/PAR-Q+ clearance if older, deconditioned, pregnant, or with a condition.

movements.name: Seated Arm Openings

cues.Open: Breathe in - sitting tall, sweep the hand up and open to the side.
cues.Gather: Breathe out - draw the hand in and down toward the lap.

staticCues: Breathe in - sitting tall, sweep the hand up and open to the side.
Breathe out - draw the hand in and down toward the lap.
movements.primary_benefit: Seated paced breathing with opening and gathering arms, for relaxation and psychological wellbeing associated with regular Tai Chi practice (R09).
movements.notes: Sit tall and stable, feet flat on the floor. Breathe in as the arm opens, out as it gathers in. A seated breath pacer, not instructed form; get clinician/PAR-Q+ clearance if older, deconditioned, pregnant, or with a condition.

movements.name: Seated Marching

cues.Lift: Breathe in - lift one foot, raising the knee; alternate legs each cycle.
cues.Lower: Breathe out - lower the foot softly back to the floor.

staticCues: Breathe in - lift one foot, raising the knee; alternate legs each cycle.
Breathe out - lower the foot softly back to the floor.
movements.primary_benefit: Seated hip-flexor and leg activation supporting standing balance; lower-limb work of this kind features in falls-prevention Tai Chi programs (R14).
movements.notes: Sit tall toward the front of a sturdy chair; lift each foot in turn to a comfortable height and alternate legs. Breathe in lifting, out lowering. A seated movement pacer, not instructed form; get clinician/PAR-Q+ clearance if older, deconditioned, pregnant, or with a condition.

movements.name: Seated Ankle & Calf Pumps

cues.Flex: Breathe in - keeping the heel down, lift the toes up toward the shin.
cues.Point: Breathe out - lift the heel and point the toes gently forward.

staticCues: Breathe in - keeping the heel down, lift the toes up toward the shin.
Breathe out - lift the heel and point the toes gently forward.
movements.primary_benefit: Seated ankle and lower-leg activation supporting standing balance and circulation; lower-limb work of this kind features in falls-prevention Tai Chi programs (R14).
movements.notes: Sit tall and stable, feet flat to start. Flex the toes up, then point them - slow and smooth, no force. Breathe in flexing, out pointing. A seated movement pacer, not instructed form; get clinician/PAR-Q+ clearance if older, deconditioned, pregnant, or with a condition.

movements.name: Gathering Qi to Close

cues.Gather Wide: Breathe in - sweep both arms wide and up, gathering the air.
cues.Draw In: Breathe out - draw the hands inward toward the chest.
cues.Rest at Dantian: Let the hands settle low at the belly, resting and still.

staticCues: Breathe in - sweep both arms wide and up, gathering the air.
Breathe out - draw the hands inward toward the chest.
Let the hands settle low at the belly, resting and still.
movements.primary_benefit: A closing breath sequence - slow, wide breathing to settle, for relaxation and psychological wellbeing associated with regular Tai Chi practice (R09).
movements.notes: Stay tall and unhurried; the breath leads the hands home to rest at the belly. Breathe in gathering wide, out drawing in and settling. A breath pacer, not instructed form; get clinician/PAR-Q+ clearance if older, deconditioned, pregnant, or with a condition.

movements.name: Slow Breathing with Arm Float

cues.Inhale Float: Breathe in slowly - let both hands float up to chest height, standing tall.
cues.Exhale Sink: Breathe out longer - let the hands sink down, soft and unhurried.

staticCues: Breathe in slowly - let both hands float up to chest height, standing tall.
Breathe out longer - let the hands sink down, soft and unhurried.
movements.primary_benefit: Slow paced breathing with a gentle arm float and a longer exhale, for relaxation and psychological wellbeing associated with regular Tai Chi practice (R09).
movements.notes: Stand tall with no knee sink; keep the exhale longer than the inhale. Breathe in as the hands float up, out as they sink. A breath pacer, not instructed form; get clinician/PAR-Q+ clearance if older, deconditioned, pregnant, or with a condition.
